data.narrative:
World Labs, a Series B AI & Software company founded by Fei-Fei Li, Justin Johnson, Christoph Lassner, and Ben Mildenhall, carries a current post-money valuation of $5.0B on $1.23B+ raised. The peer universe comprises 9 private and 1 public comparable, including Safe Superintelligence ($32.0B), Physical Intelligence ($5.6B), Nominal ($1.0B), and Photoroom ($500M), alongside Palantir (PLTR).

Two valuation methods triangulate the company's fair value. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.9x multiplier to the $5.0B last post-money valuation, implying $9.5B. Peer-median analysis across the 4 same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $3.3B, reflecting the wide dispersion in the peer set ($500M to $32.0B). The tension between these methods reflects heterogeneity in AI & Software company valuations at this stage.

Triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $2.8B to $10.9B, with a median estimate of $6.4B. The current $5.0B valuation sits near the lower quartile of this range. Key uncertainties include the sustainability of elevated AI valuations relative to peer medians and World Labs' competitive positioning within a highly disperse peer cohort. The wide peer range ($500M–$32.0B) underscores stage-specific valuation volatility.
